description_long: This object is a stylized white flower with elongated, pointed petals radiating outward from a central core, resembling a lily or similar species. The flower is supported by a sturdy, upright green stem, from which several lanceolate leaves emerge near the base. Two prominent blossoms are visible, each with a cluster of yellow-tipped stamens at the center, adding a touch of color contrast. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, overlapping slightly and creating a layered, star-like appearance. The leaves are dark green, serrated, and arch outward, providing a broad base for the plant. The overall form is naturalistic, with a slight asymmetry in the petal and leaf arrangement, typical of real flowers found in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A white, star-shaped flower with pointed petals, yellow-tipped stamens, and dark green, serrated lanceolate leaves on a single upright stem.
description_view_0: This view highlights one of the main blossoms facing forward, showing the radial arrangement of white petals and the yellow-tipped stamens at the center, with two broad green leaves visible at the base.
description_view_1: This angle reveals both blossoms clearly, displaying the layered petals and the full spread of the serrated green leaves, giving a sense of the plant's width.
description_view_2: From this side, the overlapping petals and the depth of the flower structure are visible, with the stem and leaves forming a strong vertical and horizontal base.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the back of the petals and the stem, with the leaves arching outward and the flower heads angled away, emphasizing the plant's three-dimensional form.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration around the central axis of the flower, with slight overlapping and a star-like symmetry typical of many monocot flowers.
